title: Disperis palawensis
taxonomy: Disperis palawensis is classified within the plant family Orchidaceae, commonly known as the orchid family. This family consists of approximately 30,000 species, including Disperis palawensis. The genus Disperis is further divided into several species, with Disperis palawensis being a specific species within this genus. Disperis palawensis is native to the island of Palau, which is located in the Pacific Ocean. This classification helps scientists understand the evolutionary relationships and characteristics of Disperis palawensis within its family and genus.

intro: Disperis palawensis is a species of orchid native to Palawan in the Philippines, characterized by its unique and delicate flowers. The plant has been found to possess potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may contribute to its use in traditional medicine for treating various ailments. Bioactive constituents isolated from Disperis palawensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are believed to be responsible for its medicinal properties. Disperis palawensis is typically processed into herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which are often used to support immune function and alleviate respiratory issues.
